title: Neomammillaria magallanii
taxonomy: Neomammillaria magallanii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. This succulent plant is a type of mammillaria cactus, which is native to Chile. The genus Neomammillaria is a synonym for Mammillaria, and Neomammillaria magallanii is no longer considered a valid species name. However, the plant is often referred to as Mammillaria magallanii in botanical literature. It is a small to medium-sized cactus with white flowers and is prized by cactus enthusiasts for its unique appearance.

Some species of Neomammillaria have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. intro: Neomammillaria magallanii, also known as Magallan's pincushion cactus, is a type of succulent plant native to South America, characterized by its round, fleshy stems covered in sharp spines and vibrant yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria magallanii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and skin irritations.
